title: invitations — authorization policies
description: One policy class with abilities that answer on both `sanctum` (tenant admins managing their own tenant invitations) and `platform_admin` (Academorix ops doing cross-tenant search + audit-revoke) guards. Registered by InvitationsServiceProvider::registerPolicies(). Guard-aware — each ability checks the caller's guard and gates accordingly.

abilities.name: viewAny
abilities.description: List invitations in the caller's tenant. sanctum-only — platform admins use viewAnyPlatform.

abilities.name: create
requires: invitations.send AND target permission (invitee target must accept the caller as an inviter)
abilities.description: Send a new invitation. Calls preflight validators registered per target_type by consumer modules (e.g. TenantInvitationTargetPreflight may refuse if the invitee is already a tenant member).

abilities.name: resend
requires: invitations.resend AND invitation.tenant_id === auth()->user().tenant_id AND resend_count < settings.max_resends
abilities.description: Regenerate token + re-dispatch send. Refused on terminal states + when the hard cap is hit.

abilities.name: revoke
abilities.description: Revoke a pending invitation. Refused on terminal states other than `bounced` (bounced is not truly terminal — a revoke pre-empts a retry).

abilities.name: viewAnyPlatform
abilities.description: Cross-tenant list. Filters + audit context capture on every list read (SOC 2 CC6.1).

abilities.name: revokePlatform
abilities.description: Abuse-response revoke. Requires an audit `reason`. Sets InvitationRevoked payload `isAbuseResponse=true` when reason matches an abuse pattern; that flag routes to security-team notification instead of standard tenant notification.

notes: Every ability that mutates an Invitation records an audit trail entry (via HasAuditable + a matching InvitationEvent row). Refusal (Gate::denies) is also audited under the `preflight_failed` event.

Preflight validators are consumer-supplied. Example: TenantInvitationPreflight registered by the tenancy module refuses `create` when the invitee is already a User of the target tenant. This prevents double-invitation and duplicates.
